title: Selling Digital Products Anonymously Online: What Actually Works When You Are in a Restricted Country
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: You made the product. The platform blocked you. Here is how to sell it anyway — to anyone, anywhere, with no gatekeeper in the way.

content.text: Having a great digital product to sell is only half the battle, especially when you're in a restricted country and can't use the usual payment platforms like PayPal or Stripe. You've put in the time and effort to create something valuable, but now you're stuck trying to figure out how to get paid. This is a frustrating and common problem for many digital creators, and it's one that I've seen firsthand. The good news is that there are alternatives to these traditional platforms, and they can be a game-changer for creators who have been locked out of the mainstream payment systems.
What You Are Actually Comparing

When you're looking for a way to sell your digital products, you're probably comparing different payment platforms and trying to figure out which one will work best for you. But the reality is that most of the big names in the payment processing world are not available to creators in restricted countries. This means that you're not just comparing features and fees, you're also trying to find a platform that will actually let you use it. This can be a tough and frustrating process, especially when you're just trying to get your product out into the world.
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

The traditional payment platforms like PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip all have one major flaw for creators in restricted countries: they don't work. No matter how great your product is or how much demand there is for it, you can't sell it if you can't get paid. And that's exactly what happens when you try to use one of these platforms from a restricted country. You'll be blocked at every turn, and you'll never be able to collect a single payment. This is a huge problem, and it's one that has been plaguing creators for years.
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

Crypto-native infrastructure is different because it doesn't rely on the traditional payment systems. Instead, it uses cryptocurrency to facilitate transactions, which means that you can sell your digital products to anyone, anywhere in the world, without having to worry about being blocked by a payment processor. This is a huge advantage for creators in restricted countries, because it means that they can finally start selling their products and getting paid. And with platforms like Unchained Commerce, you can even automate the delivery of your products, so you don't have to lift a finger once the sale is made.
The biggest obstacle to selling digital products is often not the product itself, but the payment processing system.
The Real Cost Difference

One of the biggest advantages of using crypto-native infrastructure is that it can be much cheaper than traditional payment platforms. With no transaction fees or middlemen to pay, you get to keep more of the money you earn from selling your digital products. This can be a huge difference for creators who are just starting out or who are operating on a tight budget. And when you combine this with the automated delivery and other features of platforms like Unchained Commerce, you can see why so many creators are making the switch to crypto-native infrastructure.
Which One to Use and Why

So which platform should you use to sell your digital products? The answer is simple: use one that works. For creators in restricted countries, this usually means using a crypto-native platform like Unchained Commerce. With its automated delivery, low fees, and ease of use, it's the perfect solution for anyone who wants to start selling their digital products and getting paid. And with the Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, you have everything you need to start selling and growing your business.
What Creators in Restricted Countries Are Asking

If you're wondering how to sell an ebook without using Gumroad or Payhip, the answer is to use a crypto-native platform that allows you to automate the delivery of your product. This way, you can sell your ebook to anyone, anywhere in the world, without having to worry about being blocked by a payment processor. The same is true for selling online courses without a Stripe account: you just need to find a platform that works for you and your customers. And when it comes to delivering digital products automatically, the key is to use a platform that has this feature built-in, like Unchained Commerce.
Crypto-native infrastructure is the key to unlocking true platform independence and reaching a global customer base.
Related Topics Worth Reading

For creators who want to sell ebooks, courses, templates, and other digital products, the ability to accept crypto payments and automate delivery is a game-changer. It means that you can finally start selling your products and getting paid, without having to worry about being blocked by a payment processor. And with the rise of crypto-native infrastructure, it's never been easier to get started and start growing your business.
Your Launch Checklist — No Platform Permission Required

Before you launch your digital product store, make sure you have a few key things in place. First, you need a platform that can handle automated delivery of your products, like the Core Shop Engine from Unchained Commerce. You also need a way to connect with your customers' wallets, which is where Web3 Wallet Connect comes in. And finally, you need a way to track your sales and analytics, which is where the Admin Dashboard comes in. With these tools in place, you'll be ready to start selling and growing your business in no time.
Success as a digital creator means being able to sell your products and get paid, without having to worry about being blocked by a payment processor.
